questions.scenario: A worker writes generated invoices to `/tmp/invoices` inside the running container and support loses files after a restart. The files must be downloaded later by customers. What change fits the article?

options.text: Store the generated files in Blob Storage and keep lookup metadata in the database.
Increase container memory so the temporary directory has more space.
Move the files into Cosmos DB because restart loss proves they are key-value data.
Attach a managed disk to each container replica so every replica has local durable files.

questions.explanation: Temporary runtime storage is not a durable application store. Generated downloadable files usually belong in Blob Storage with metadata stored separately.

questions.scenario: A migration plan keeps a legacy shared folder for two months while a new Blob Storage path is rolled out. Which details should be written into the release record?

options.text: The file share name, storage account, quota, and protocol.
Which workers still depend on the share and when they will stop writing to it.
Snapshot or backup evidence for the share during the migration.
A statement that shared folders are old, so they can be deleted after the next deploy.

questions.explanation: The release record should show what still uses the share and how it can be recovered. Age alone is not a deletion plan.
